Title: MC Thanh Thao: Many times had to hold back tears to keep the story moving when hosting "Vietnamese Family Warm Home"
Content:
As a familiar face of the program Vietnamese Family Shelter, MC Thanh Thao takes on the role of connection between characters, artists and the audience. After nearly two years leading the program, she not only conveys content but also regulates emotions throughout each episode.
As the host of the Vietnamese Family Home program, MC Thanh Thao has brought the audience many memorable moments
When emotions are "material" but also a challenge
Thanh Thao participated in the program as a guest before becoming the official MC. According to her, the biggest difference between the two positions is the way they deal with emotions. As a guest, the artist can burn with all his emotions to overcome challenges and bring rewards to the children. But with MC, the mission doesn't stop at participating. The host must observe, listen, connect and keep the story moving.
Thanh Thao said she could not "act" her emotions and did not know "what to do to cry well or laugh well". Everything comes naturally. But that naturalness poses a strict requirement: self-control.
"As a guest, I can cry a lot and be so emotional that I can't put it into words. But as an MC, if I let my emotions take me too far and cry too much, I will lose the flow of the story."
she confided.
“Every situation is pitiful, every character makes me tormented.” – MC Thanh Thao shared
That's why in many moments, when watching clips introducing the character's situation, Thanh Thao had to "hold herself back" - press her hands, tighten her body to stop the tears. Not because she is insensitive, but because she understands that, if the host cannot maintain balance, the story will easily fall into tragedy instead of spreading human values.
Nearly two years with the Vietnamese Family Shelter program as an MC, Thanh Thao has listened to hundreds of different situations. But to her, no story is more "familiar" or "comfortable". Because
“Every situation is pitiful, every character makes me tormented.”
she shared.
Not stopping at comforting and encouraging the children during the recording process, Thanh Thao also actively sought out information about the characters in advance. She contacted her family and friends, calling for support so she could bring small but practical contributions to the children.
There are days, before the cameras even start, she already has tens of millions of donated dong in her hand. It is not only material, but also mental preparation, as a way to stay motivated in difficult situations.
"Some people tease us that we are artists who often 'ask for money'. But if it's to help children, then a little 'notoriety' is okay."
Thanh Thao said, half joking and half serious.
The concerns behind the decision to stick with Viet Family Shelter
Before accepting the invitation to lead the program, Thanh Thao could not avoid hesitation. Unlike many other television programs, Vietnamese Family Shelter often records in provinces and cities, in weather conditions that are not always favorable.
At a time when I have a stable career and am running a skills training center, constantly moving is no small challenge. Her family, especially her husband, was very worried about her health. However, after directly witnessing the humane values ​​that the program brings, he fully supported it.
There is always an energetic MC Thanh Thao at Viet Family Shelter
"There were times when I was very tired on the bus, but when I got to the filming location, I felt like I had more energy,"
MC
Thanh Thao shared. For her, this is not just a job but also a meaningful part of her 26-year career journey.
Thanh Thao also often shares with her children about the situations she has encountered. Instead of imposing, she chose to talk to her children to understand that they are luckier than many other children, thereby appreciating their family, being conscious of their own efforts, and living more responsibly.
The journey of spreading love and educational values
One of the things that touched Thanh Thao the most was the audience's reaction at the recording sessions. Many people initially just came to watch, but when they witnessed the situation, the character couldn't hold back their tears and was ready to contribute.
She told very real life stories: a fire fighter on duty still enlisting his support; A lottery ticket seller, even though he didn't know what his income was that day, was still willing to share. These actions, although small, have great spiritual value, proving that the spirit of "good leaves protect torn leaves" is always present in life.
MC Thanh Thao accepts "the reputation of asking for money" if it helps the children
MC Thanh Thao said that she often takes time to read the audience's comments after each broadcast episode. There, she saw sympathy and changes in viewers' perceptions. Many people shared that, even though they were not very complete, when looking at the circumstances in the program, they realized they were still lucky. And from there, they want to make more efforts, not only to improve their personal lives, but also to be able to help others. According to Thanh Thao, that's nine

Title: More than 2.6 billion VND was awarded after 3 days of filming "Vietnamese Family Home" in Ho Chi Minh City
Content:
Closing 3 days of filming in Tam Thang ward, Ho Chi Minh City (formerly Vung Tau), the program "Vietnamese Family Home" gave more than 2.6 billion VND to children in difficult circumstances.
The Vietnamese family home received a lot of love from people in the city. HCM after more than 2 years returned
After nearly 4 years of traveling all over the country, "Vietnamese Family Home" officially returned to filming in Ho Chi Minh City after more than 2 years, bringing many emotions during the 3 days taking place at the Sports Stadium - 607 Nguyen An Ninh, Tam Thang ward.
With the companionship of Hoa Sen Group, and the cooperation of guest artists, sponsors and local audiences, the program awarded a total amount of more than 2.6 billion VND to 18 families with extremely difficult circumstances in the area. In particular, the prize money from sponsors Hoa Sen Home Construction Materials & Furniture Supermarket System and Hoa Sen Plastic Pipes - Source of happiness is
594 million rumors
g
. In addition, artists, audiences and sponsors contributed more than 2 billion VND directly at the recording location.
The artists couldn't help but get choked up at the story of the child's family
Right from the moment he appeared, actor Duy Khuong brought a vibrant atmosphere with humor and positive energy. However, when watching the characters' situations, he could not hide his emotions and burst into tears because he sympathized with the deprivations they had to go through.
The actor shared that he also grew up without a father and was raised by his mother. Talking about the years of living in a rundown rented house, where her mother had to work hard to raise three small children, Duy Khuong was speechless.
MC Dai Nghia expressed his emotion and surprise when he first heard his colleagues share about their childhood. He said that although they often work together, this is a rare time when Duy Khuong shares his feelings deeply. The male MC was impressed by his colleague's determination to improve, not only changing his own life but also spreading positive energy to everyone.
At the program, Duy Khuong expressed:
"I am the father of 5 children, so I understand the hearts of parents even more. I feel lucky to participate in the program, to contribute to sharing and accompanying children who are facing difficulties."
Runner-up Do Cam Ly could not hide her sadness when witnessing the characters' situations. What worries her is not only the lack of material resources, but also the early maturity of children. Besides sharing her feelings, the queen tries her best in challenges to bring rewards to the families.
As an MC, Thanh Thao many times choked up at the image of elderly grandmothers still having to make a living to raise their children and take care of their grandchildren. In particular, the story of Truc Mai (born in 2010, Ho Chi Minh City) living with her grandmother who sells lottery tickets, sometimes having to eat rice and salt for meals, made the female MC feel sad.
MC Duong Hong Phuc also burst into tears many times while watching the characters' stories. As a father, he was even more poignant when he witnessed Cam Dao (born in 2013, Lam Dong) chatting with his father's portrait. The male MC gave me a hug of encouragement and sincere words of comfort.
Guest artists sold goods to raise nearly 30 million VND to donate to the children
During the challenges, rapper Phuc Du and singer My My completed the squid box and steamed milk crab competitions, then joined MC Duong Hong Phuc to sell products to raise funds. With the support of the audience, the artists collected nearly 30 million VND to send to the families.
Phuc Du shared that he deeply sympathized with the children's situation, because he also lost his father early, and his mother earned a living by selling bread to support their children. Memories of his hard-working mother made the male rapper unable to hold back his tears.
My My also felt emotional when remembering her mother - a woman who always hid her illness to take care of her children. The female singer sent words of encouragement to the mothers and grandmothers in the program, hoping everyone would stay healthy to continue being a support for their families.
For the second time participating in the program, singer Ly Ly caused emotions when composing the song "Vietnamese Family Home" specifically for the program. After performing, she decided to hand over all copyrights to the song to accompany the charity journey. Before this gesture, the representative of Hoa Sen Group donated 30 million VND, and Ly Ly continued to give this amount to the 3 families participating in the filming.
During the challenges, Ly Ly and actor Vinh Dam tried their best to bring rewards to the children. Singer Liz Kim Cuong, participating in the final recording session, was also touched by the children's situation and actively participated in the challenge to contribute to supporting the families.
MC Oc Thanh Van, singer Liz Kim Cuong and football player Pham Ly Duc offered words of encouragement and comfort to the families of children in difficult circumstances.
Meritorious Artist Oc Thanh Van once again expressed her sorrow for children who have to grow up too early. Even though she was busy with the schedule of interacting with the film crew as well as participating in theater performances, the female artist still took the opportunity to accept the invitation to host the program. As soon as the filming ended, she immediately got on the bus to return to participate in the stage performance.
In addition, Oc Thanh Van also joined football player Pham Ly Duc and singer Liz

Title: Overcoming adversity from the pages of old books, a 16-year-old male student won the National Excellent Student award
Content:
The first filming day of "Vietnamese Family Home" in Ho Chi Minh City brought many emotions, from tears at difficult circumstances to joy when the children received practical support from artists and audiences.
Actor Do Nhat Hoang and Singer Muoi accompanied the first day of filming in the city. HCM
On April 24, the program "Vietnamese Family Home" officially recorded the first day at the Sports Stadium - No. 607 Nguyen An Ninh, Tam Thang Ward, Ho Chi Minh City, with the participation of Meritorious Artist - MC Dai Nghia, actor - singer Song Luan, singer Anh Tu, actor Do Nhat Hoang and singer Muoi.
In the hot weather conditions of early summer, a large number of audiences in Tam Thang ward, Ho Chi Minh City (Vung Tau City area, old Ba Ria - Vung Tau province) still showed up early to watch the program, accompany and support children in difficult circumstances. On the first day of filming in Tam Thang ward, the program brought many emotions, from tears at the disadvantaged circumstances of the children to joy when the children received meaningful support. Each moment contributes to portraying the compassionate journey that "Vietnamese Family Home" has persistently carried out over the years.
The story of the 16-year-old boy's determination to win the National Excellent Student award impressed all three artists
Even though he has hosted the program many times, MC Dai Nghia still cannot hide his sadness when listening to the characters' stories. Not only was he heartbroken by the lack of both material and spirit, the male MC was also touched by the shares that showed the children's early maturity. On the day of filming, Huu Tri (born in 2009, from Dong Thap) was determined to self-study through textbooks to have the opportunity to join the team of excellent students and win prizes:
Consolation prize for Excellent Student at the National level in Geography, First Prize for Excellent Student at the Provincial level in Geography
. With the desire to reduce the economic burden on my family, I always study hard. I don't have money for extra classes, but I try to explore and improve my knowledge on my own, making Dai Nghia choke up.
As the only female artist participating in filming on the first day, singer Muoi still tried her best in the challenges. The female singer expressed her admiration for an older sister who dared to put aside her college dreams to go to work, raising her younger sister's dream of going to school. From then on, she told herself she would do her best, not afraid of challenges, even willing to throw herself into the water to complete the task, contributing to bringing more rewards to the characters.
For actor Do Nhat Hoang, participating in the program is an opportunity to directly share with children who suffer many disadvantages early in life. He expressed his appreciation for accompanying artists, spreading positive values ​​to the community.
Singer Anh Tu sells cakes to earn money to help children during filming
Similarly, actor - singer Song Luan and singer Anh Tu took advantage of the baking challenge to sell products to raise funds to support difficult situations. MC Dai Nghia also joined the guests, directly "selling" to contribute more support for the character. This activity received a strong response from local audiences, many benefactors were willing to buy cakes for 2 million, 5 million, even 6 million VND per portion. The emotional "payment" scenes made Song Luan surprised. In just a short time, the artists sold all 16 boxes of sponge cakes, earning nearly 30 million VND to send to the characters.
Singer Anh Tu shared that it has been a long time since he had the opportunity to be so close to the audience. He expressed his emotion and gratitude at having the opportunity to join the audience in spreading the spirit of sharing and joining hands to support difficult situations. Faced with stories full of loss, Anh Tu continuously encouraged the children and at the same time tried his best in challenges to bring back practical rewards, contributing to helping them overcome difficulties in life.
The first filming day of "Vietnamese Family Home" in Ho Chi Minh City brought many emotions

Title: "Vietnamese Family Home" filmed in Ho Chi Minh City, artists join hands to support 18 disadvantaged children
Content:
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program will return to Ho Chi Minh City with 3 days of filming, gathering many artists to overcome challenges and join hands to support 18 children in difficult circumstances in the Southeast and Central Highlands regions.
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program will kick off its filming journey in Ho Chi Minh City for 3 days (April 24–26, 2026). Recording location at the Sports Stadium - No. 607 Nguyen An Ninh, Tam Thang Ward, Ho Chi Minh City (Vung Tau City, former Ba Ria - Vung Tau province).
Many famous artists will participate in filming "Vietnamese Family Home" in Ho Chi Minh City
The program is hosted by Meritorious Artist - MC Dai Nghia, MC Duong Hong Phuc, MC Thanh Thao, Meritorious Artist - MC Oc Thanh Van, with the participation of guests: actor Duy Khuong, actor - singer Song Luan, singer Anh Tu, singer Liz Kim Cuong, rapper - musician Phuc Du, singer LyLy, singer My My, actor Do Nhat Hoang, singer Muoi, runner-up Do Cam Ly, player Pham Ly Duc and actor Vinh Dam. Everyone will join together to overcome challenges to support families in difficult circumstances.
As one of the guests in this recording, singer Muoi shared that she had followed the program for a long time and was impressed with the children's energy.
"Even though their circumstances are very poor, they still maintain optimism, have dreams and always try to realize their dreams. That makes me very emotional. I feel happy and excited to have the opportunity to accompany and share with children who have difficult circumstances but have the will to rise."
– the female singer shared.
Actor Do Nhat Hoang said that for him, this is not only a meaningful activity but also an opportunity to directly listen and share with children. The actor expressed his hope that his companionship can contribute to energizing and motivating the children to be more steadfast on the journey ahead. He also hopes that the audience will come watch and spread the good values ​​that the program brings.
For runner-up Do Cam Ly, each story in the show is a source of inspiration for the will to live, love for family and faith in life. The queen said that when watching the program on television, she could not help but be moved by the optimistic spirit of the children, even in the most difficult circumstances. Being able to directly participate, listen and share makes her feel happy as she can contribute to giving more confidence to the children.
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program always receives a lot of love and sharing from audiences across the country
After nearly 4 years of traveling all over the country,
Vietnamese family warm home
officially returned to Ho Chi Minh City, continuing the journey of "Bringing happiness to share with the community" in the Southeast and Central Highlands regions. This comeback is not only a recording session, but also 3 days of spreading love, accompanying 18 difficult people from Ho Chi Minh City, Dong Nai, Tay Ninh and Lam Dong.
Not stopping at the rewards in each episode, the production crew and sponsor Hoa Sen Group also continue to accompany the characters long after the show. Recently, Hoa Sen Group donated roofs and toilets to Vi Thi Vien's family (episode 152) and Huynh Thi Mai Phuong's family (episode 171). Activities such as house repair, corrugated iron roofing, and toilet construction are implemented regularly, contributing to helping families stabilize their lives and look forward to a better future.
The family of Huynh Thi Mai Phuong - the character who appeared in episode 171 - received Hoa Sen Group's promise to help renovate the new toilet.

Title: 18 children in difficult circumstances were supported during the filming of "Vietnamese Family Home" in Ho Chi Minh City
Content:
The program "Vietnamese Family Home" officially returned to filming in Ho Chi Minh City, continuing the journey of "Bringing happiness to share with the community" in the Southeast and Central Highlands regions.
After nearly 4 years of traveling through many provinces and cities across the country, the program "Vietnamese Family Home" will be filmed from April 24 to 26, 2026 in Tam Thang ward, marking the return to Ho Chi Minh City after more than 2 years. This recording aims to support 18 especially difficult situations from Ho Chi Minh City, Dong Nai, Tay Ninh and Lam Dong.
The journey to find the filming character took place from March 16 to March 30 in the Southeast and Central Highlands regions.
To prepare for the organization, the Department of Culture and Sports of Ho Chi Minh City chaired a working session with the sponsor, Hoa Sen Group Joint Stock Company and the production unit - Bee Communications Company. At the meeting, Ms. Tran Thi Thu Hien - Deputy Director of Ho Chi Minh City Department of Culture and Sports highly appreciated the humanity of the program, and expressed her joy when Tam Thang ward was chosen as the filming location. Department leaders hope that the program will continue to spread the spirit of sharing, contributing to supporting children in difficult circumstances to have more learning opportunities.
Ho Chi Minh City Department of Culture and Sports worked with the Sponsor and Producer of the Vietnamese Family Home program to prepare for filming in Tam Thang ward.
Immediately after that, the "Vietnamese Family Home" team urgently deployed the work of searching and surveying characters in the Southeast and Central Highlands regions. The survey process is carefully conducted to select the right situations that truly need support, ensuring transparency and fairness with the spirit of not leaving anyone behind.
With close coordination between local authorities, sponsors and production units, the filming in Tam Thang ward is expected to leave many good marks in Ho Chi Minh City. The program continues to affirm its role as a bridge of love, where compassionate hearts join hands to light up hope for less fortunate children.
In particular, the program is led by
Meritorious Artist – MC Dai Nghia, MC Duong Hong Phuc, MC Thanh Thao, Meritorious Artist – MC Oc Thanh Van
and the presence of famous guests:
Actor Duy Khuong, actor - singer Song Luan, singer Anh Tu, singer Liz Kim Cuong, rapper - musician Phuc Du, singer LyLy, singer My My, actor Do Nhat Hoang, singer Muoi, runner-up Cam Ly, player Pham Ly Duc and actor Vinh Dam
will join hands to overcome the challenges of the program to help families in difficult circumstances.
"Vietnamese Family Home" has come a long way through more than 30 provinces and cities across the country.
Before returning to Ho Chi Minh City, the program "Vietnamese Family Home" had a long journey across 30 provinces and cities across the country. With the companionship of more than 300 artists and the support of millions of local audiences, the program has awarded nearly 85 billion VND. Of which, the bonus amount from Hoa Sen Group is nearly 18 billion VND, supporting 546 families, with more than 1,000 children in difficult circumstances across the country.
Besides the value of material support, the "Vietnamese Family Home" program also contributes to nurturing the spirit of solidarity in the community, especially the younger generation. Through authentic and emotional stories, the program creates connections between difficult situations with philanthropists, businesses and audiences across the country. This return to Ho Chi Minh City is expected to continue to further arouse the spirit of sharing, spread positive values ​​and inspire many people to join hands for a humane, sustainable society.
"Vietnamese Family Home" always receives enthusiastic support from local audiences.

Title: The journey of the Vietnamese Family Shelter in Dak Lak ended, giving more than 1.5 billion VND to 18 disadvantaged families.
Content:
Closing 3 days of filming in Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province, the program "Vietnamese Family Home" gave more than 1.5 billion VND to difficult situations in the Central region and Central Highlands.
The program's second journey to Dak Lak province, and also the first time filming was held in the old Phu Yen area, just ended with many emotions. With the companionship of Hoa Sen Group, along with the support of guest artists, numerous sponsors and local audiences, the program gave a total amount of more than 1.5 billion VND to 18 families of orphans and children in particularly difficult circumstances in the Central and Central Highlands provinces.
The "Vietnamese Family Home" journey in Dak Lak ended, giving more than 1.5 billion VND to 18 difficult families.
In particular, the prize money from the sponsors Hoa Sen Home Construction Materials & Furniture Supermarket System and Hoa Sen Plastic Pipes - Source of Happiness given to the characters is 600 million VND. In addition, the cooperation of a large number of local audiences and sponsors who came to watch and support the program and the artists contributed more than 900 million VND to support difficult lives.
Appearing full of energy at the recording session, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu still couldn't hide his tears when witnessing the characters' plight. The male singer is saddened by the children's understanding that at a very young age, they have to face emotional deprivation and at the same time shoulder family responsibilities early. The image of families with only grandparents and grandchildren relying on each other and the lack of meals made him feel sad.
Cris Phan - Mai Quynh Anh returned to their hometown to participate in the program, sharing and bringing joy to the children.
Actors Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh joined the program as guests. Mai Quynh Anh shared her happiness when accompanying the program right in her hometown (former Phu Yen) - where she was born and raised. Cris Phan said he had watched the show before and couldn't hide his emotions when listening to the stories about the characters' situations. The actor was especially impressed with the image of a large audience present at the recording session, not only to meet the artist but also to directly donate cash and in-kind support to the families. He said he and his wife would try their best in the challenges to help the children bring home rewards from Hoa Sen Group, and hoped the audience would continue to accompany and share with the children to overcome difficulties.
The plight of three ethnic sisters who lost their parents made Hung Nguyen and Gigi Huong Giang choked up.
After about two years, athlete Truong Dinh Hoang returned to "Vietnamese Family Home" during a recording session in Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province. He said he always wanted to return to the program to accompany and share with children in difficult circumstances. When he learned that the program would be recorded in the old Phu Yen area, the male athlete was even more excited to participate. Born and raised in Dak Lak, and having had many activities to support people in Phu Yen during storms and floods, he sees this participation as an opportunity to contribute to his homeland.
Even though he had just had a traffic accident, Truong Dinh Hoang still did his best in the challenge with Mai Phuong, bringing joy to the children.
At the recording session, Truong Dinh Hoang could not hide his emotions when witnessing difficult circumstances. Even though he had just experienced a traffic accident that resulted in three broken ribs, he still decided to participate after a period of consideration, with the desire to contribute to sharing with children who are in need both materially and mentally.
Also a returning guest on the show, runner-up Huynh Pham Thuy Tien expressed her wish to have more opportunities to accompany "Vietnamese Family Home" to continue sharing with difficult families. The queen has prepared gifts of learning kits, with the hope of motivating the children to study hard and aim for a better future.
Accompanying runner-up Thuy Tien during the recording session, singer Khai Dang shared his happiness when participating in the program. The male singer said he was touched many times while watching "Vietnamese Family Home", especially impressed with the children's determination to rise. According to him, it is the students' spirit of not giving up that has become the motivation for him to do his best in challenges.
During the competitions of the program, Khai Dang and Thuy Tien did not hesitate to work hard and together brought meaningful gifts to the children.
True to their promise when coming to the program, Khai Dang and Thuy Tien were not afraid to show off, regardless of their image in the competitions. Even though they were covered in powder, the two still tried their best to complete the mission, bringing meaningful rewards to the characters.

Title: Double2T, Phi Phuong Anh, Cris Phan - Mai Quynh Anh and many artists participating in "Vietnamese Family Home" in Tuy Hoa, Dak Lak
Content:
Meritorious Artist Dai Nghia, Meritorious Artist Oc Thanh Van, boxer Truong Dinh Hoang, rapper Double2T, artist couple, actors Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh and many artists will be present in Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province to support 18 children in difficult circumstances in the Vietnamese Family Shelter program.
A group of Vietnamese artists gathered in Tuy Hoa to film "Vietnamese Family Home" in 3 days
Program
Vietnamese family warm home
will be filming in Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province for 3 days (March 20–22, 2026). The filming location is Dien Hong Park, No. 1 Tran Hung Dao Street, Tuy Hoa Ward, Dak Lak Province. The program brings together many Vietnamese artists to participate in filming and support 18 orphans and children in extremely difficult circumstances from provinces and regions such as Dak Lak, Gia Lai, Khanh Hoa and Lam Dong.
The program is hosted by MCs: Meritorious Artists Dai Nghia, Duong Hong Phuc, Thanh Thao, Meritorious Artists Oc Thanh Van and Thai Vu. In addition, there will be the presence of many guest artists such as: boxer Truong Dinh Hoang, singer GiGi Huong Giang, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu, singer Phuong Anh Bolero, rapper Double2T, singer Phi Phuong Anh, singer Khai Dang, male king Hung Nguyen, runner-up Huynh Pham Thuy Tien, singer Mai Phuong and artist-actress couple Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh.
MCs and guest artists recorded for 3 days at Dien Hong Park, Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province
King Hung Nguyen confirmed his participation in the program and expressed his happiness at having the opportunity to participate in the recording for the first time. He shared that in the past, when watching the broadcast episodes of the program, he was always moved by the difficult situations as well as the spirit of sharing that the program spread. Therefore, when he received the invitation to participate, he felt very honored and appreciated the opportunity to accompany him in this special recording.
Hung Nguyen said he will bring all his strength and enthusiasm to the program, ready to face challenges to contribute to supporting difficult situations. Besides, he also hopes that the audience in Tuy Hoa city will come watch, cheer and support the program, thereby spreading care and love to families in need of help.
Many audiences will have the opportunity to witness the artists' challenges and participate in meaningful activities for children.
Singer Mai Phuong said that besides being emotional when watching and participating in the program, she especially admired the strength of the children appearing in each episode. According to the female singer, even though they face many difficulties in life, they still strive to rise up, keep their dreams and study hard towards a better future. It is that strong spirit that makes her appreciate the humane values of the program more
Vietnamese family warm home
bring.
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program promises to attract a lot of attention from the people of Dak Lak and neighboring provinces and cities.
Mai Phuong also expressed her hope that her participation in this recording could somewhat motivate the children, helping them feel the care and love from the community. At the same time, she hopes that through the program, meaningful stories and positive energy will be spread more widely, so that more and more people will join hands to share and help difficult situations.
On his personal page, boxer Truong Dinh Hoang shared:
"This is the second time Hoang has accompanied the Vietnamese Family Shelter program. Each time he participates, Hoang appreciates more the values that the program brings to children in difficult circumstances. This time participating, Hoang will try his best in the challenges to bring back many meaningful rewards, contributing a little more motivation and confidence to the children on their journey ahead."
Hoa Sen Home and Hoa Sen Plastic Pipe bring product experience space and post-Tet incentives to local audiences
In parallel with the filming program "Vietnamese Family Home", visitors also have the opportunity to experience the shopping space at Hoa Sen Home Container Showroom, taking place from March 16 to March 22. The display space is modernly designed, providing opportunities for customers to directly visit, learn and experience many quality construction materials and household equipment products, from bathroom and kitchen equipment, tiles, glazed tiles to paint, electrical equipment and construction tools from the Hoa Sen Home ecosystem and many reputable brands on the market.
Visitors have the opportunity to experience the modern shopping space at Hoa Sen Home Container Showroom.
Notably, during the event, Hoa Sen Home launched a promotion program with discounts of up to 35% for more than 800 product codes in many industries such as bricks, tiles, kitchen equipment, bathroom equipment, paint and construction materials. In addition, the "Buy 1 Get 1 Free" program is also applied to some products of the Lushine kitchen equipment and Tuslo bathroom equipment brands.

Title: 18 extremely difficult situations were supported during the filming of "Vietnamese Family Home" in Dak Lak
Content:
Right after the Lunar New Year of the Horse, the program "Vietnamese Family Home" continues its journey to carry out the mission of "Bringing happiness to share with the community" in the provinces of the South Central Coast and Central Highlands.
Opening the new year of Binh Ngo 2026, the program officially selected Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province as the first stop. With the participation of many guest artists, "Vietnamese Family Home" promises to bring practical values ​​to local people and neighboring provinces.
"Vietnamese Family Home" continues its journey of spreading love in the South Central Coast and Central Highlands regions after the Lunar New Year of the Horse.
During 3 days, from March 20 to 22, 2026, the program will be recorded at
Dien Hong Park, Tan Da Street, Tuy Hoa Ward, Dak Lak Province
, to support 18 especially difficult situations from the provinces of Dak Lak, Gia Lai, Khanh Hoa and Lam Dong. The characters of this journey are orphans who lack the arms of their parents, or children who still have parents but their families are in difficult situations and their parents have lost their ability to work. Through challenges and support from sponsors, the program hopes to provide funding to help children continue to study, stabilize their lives and nurture their future dreams.
To prepare for the filming, the Department of Culture, Sports and Tourism of Dak Lak province chaired a meeting between the locality and the sponsor Hoa Sen Group Joint Stock Company and the main production unit - Bee Media Company. At the meeting, leaders of Dak Lak province highly appreciated the humanity of the program and expressed their joy when Hoa Sen Group chose the locality as the location for the filming. The provincial representative also hopes that through the program, the spirit of love and sharing will continue to spread, contributing to supporting children in particularly difficult circumstances.
Working session between the Department of Culture, Sports and Tourism of Dak Lak province with sponsor Hoa Sen Group and the production unit of the program "Vietnamese Family Home".
Immediately after the meeting, the "Vietnamese Family Home" team urgently deployed the search and survey of characters in the South Central Coast and Central Highlands areas before the Lunar New Year of the Horse. The survey process is carried out carefully to ensure the correct selection of situations that truly need support, ensuring transparency and fairness.
The filming took place right after Tet, which has even more special meaning. When spring opens a new beginning, the support from the program is considered "lucky money" at the beginning of the year, helping children have more conditions to cover their lives, renovate their homes, invest in their studies and confidently move forward. This is also how the program demonstrates long-term companionship, not just temporary financial support.
The companionship of local audiences contributes to the success of each stop of the program.
With close coordination between local authorities, sponsors and production units, the filming in Tuy Hoa ward, Dak Lak province is expected to leave many good marks. More than one television program, "Vietnamese Family Home" continues to affirm its role as a bridge of love, where compassionate hearts meet to light up hope for less fortunate children across the country.

Title: Hoa Sen Group continues to maintain its winning streak at the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards for 3 consecutive years
Content:
At the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards - Van Xuan Awards 2025, Viet Family Shelter and Hoa Sen Steel Sheet continued to be named, and made their mark in two important categories. This achievement clearly affirms the communication effectiveness and persistent spread of the human values ​​that Hoa Sen Group persistently cultivates for the community.
On the evening of December 19, at Nguyen Hue Walking Street (Ho Chi Minh City), the awards ceremony of the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards 2025 - Van Xuan Awards 2025 took place excitingly, marking a breakthrough season with more than 1,500 entries from nearly 200 Brands, Agencies, Production houses and individuals in the creative industry. In 2025, the Award continues to position groundbreaking, new and full of national pride values, opening up a journey to find identity through a creative lens with the spirit of "Vietnamese Pride".
In that general picture, Hoa Sen Group marked a notable milestone when it entered the third consecutive year of being named at the Van Xuan Awards. This year, the Vietnamese Family Shelter program and Hoa Sen Steel Sheet brand continued to win double awards in two important categories respectively: "Most impressive CSR campaign" and "Most loved brand", thereby showing the persistence in the communication strategy associated with the business's community values.
Mr. Tran Dinh Tai - Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group, Mr. Tran Dinh Phuong - Deputy General Director of Bee Communications Company and actors Lam Vy Da and Huynh Lap at the Van Xuan Awards 2025 ceremony.
The award "Most impressive CSR campaign 2025" continues to extend the chain of glorious achievements of Vietnamese Family Shelter
2025 is the third consecutive year the program has been named at this prestigious award. Accordingly, the Vietnamese Family Shelter program won a double title at the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards - Van Xuan Awards 2024 in the two categories "Most Favorite Creative Brand" and "Most Impressive CSR Campaign". And in the 2023 season, Viet Family Shelter also won impressively when being honored in two categories "Brand with meaningful social activities/Best brand for the community" and "Integrated media advertising".
The Vietnamese family home is one of the most prominent highlights in Hoa Sen Group's CSR activities. The program is highly appreciated for its honest approach, focusing on the characters' emotional everyday stories. This is a humanitarian journey following more than 10 years of volunteering by Hoa Sen Group. After more than three years of broadcasting in the form of reality television, the program has gone through 28/34 provinces and cities, supporting more than 500 families to promptly overcome adversity, giving away nearly 71 billion VND and following in the footsteps of more than 1,000 children to continue going to school.
As the hosts of the program Vietnamese Family Shelter, actors Lam Vy Da and Huynh Lap brought the audience many memorable moments.
As one of the guest artists who became the host of the program "Vietnamese Family Home", actor Lam Vy Da shared: "
For me, being able to accompany Viet Family Shelter is a very special opportunity. This award is recognition for the persistent journey of spreading humanistic values of Hoa Sen Group, the program crew and the artists. Hopefully the program will continue to bring faith, hope and spiritual support to many difficult situations across the country."
Accompanying the program in the hosting role, actor Huynh Lap also expressed many emotions when Vietnamese Family Shelter continued to be honored:
"Lat believes that the greatest value of the program lies not only in the numbers or awards, but in the fact that each story told touches the hearts of viewers and spreads the spirit of sharing. The fact that the program and Hoa Sen Group are continuously recognized at a prestigious award like the Van Xuan Awards is a great source of encouragement for the entire crew, artists and companions to continue their efforts on the journey for the community. Lap hopes that Viet Family Home will continue to do so." far, so that more difficult situations can be strengthened and more faith can be ignited.”
Deserved recognition for the brand's community journey
If Viet Family Shelter is considered a clear testament to humanity in creativity, the "Most Favorite Brand" award for Hoa Sen Steel Sheet  reflects public recognition for a brand with a long-term attachment to the community, and also shows trust in the ecosystem that Hoa Sen Group is building.  This love is formed through many years of presence and accompanying people with practical activities.

Hoa Sen Home Construction Materials and Furniture Supermarket System

Title: Vietnamese family home makes its mark at the Human Act Prize 2025 with the title "Inspirational Project"
Content:
On the evening of December 16, 2025, at Hoan Kiem Lake Theater, Hanoi, the Human Act Prize 2025 Award Ceremony - Community Action Award 2025 took place in a solemn and emotional atmosphere. At the event, Viet Family Shelter was honored to be named in the "Inspirational Project" category, marking the humanitarian journey and practical values ​​that the program has been spreading widely in the community.
Human Act Prize is a national-level award, hosted by Nhan Dan Newspaper in coordination with VCCorp Joint Stock Company, to honor individuals and organizations that have made positive contributions to the community through projects that create sustainable social impact. In 2025, the award takes the theme "Perseverance in serving", recognizing steadfast and persistent actions in serving the community, although sometimes silently, they spread far and wide and have a lasting impact.
This year's Final Jury Council gathered many prestigious domestic and international figures, representatives of ministries, departments, branches and leaders of large organizations. From more than 27 typical projects selected for the final round, project representatives directly presented and answered questions to the Board of Examiners before the official results were announced.
At the awards night, the Community Action Awards Council awarded the Inspiration category with 5 projects: Vietnamese Family Home, Hair Library, Love in the Wave, Lighting a Match, Coloring Memories-Skyline.
In the general picture of models creating sustainable social impact, Vietnamese Family Shelter makes its mark thanks to its honest, people-centered approach. The program focuses on depicting emotional everyday stories, reflecting families' strength to overcome adversity and children's desire to continue going to school, thereby conveying profound humanistic messages and creating a positive influence in the community. Title: The title of "Marketing Community creating great impact" at Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025 belongs to Viet Family Shelter
Content:
At the Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025 taking place within the framework of the Vietnam Marketing Day event on December 12 at the Continental Saigon Hotel, the Vietnamese Family Home program was honored to be named in the category Marketing Community Creates Great Impact.
Vietnam Marketing Awards
initiated by the Vietnam Marketing Association in 2025 to build creative - effective - humane marketing professional standards. The category "Community Marketing creates a big impact" is for community media projects that create sustainable value and have a clear impact on society. The fact that Vietnamese Family Shelter was selected for this title is recognition of the marketing orientation that Hoa Sen Group pursues: creating real value and sustainable development through companionship with the community.
In addition, the title "Marketing Community with a Great Impact" at Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025 is an important mark in Hoa Sen Group's humanitarian journey and is also the foundation for Vietnamese Family Home to continue to bring happiness to share with the community in the coming time. This is recognition for a long journey associated with social responsibility, reaching out to the community with activities of real value and showing that the Marketing approach comes from humanity.
Vietnamese family warm home
carried out by Bee Communications Company in collaboration with Ho Chi Minh City Radio and Television Station, with the companionship of Hoa Sen Home Construction Materials & Furniture Supermarket System (Hoa Sen Group) and Hoa Sen Plastic Pipes - Source of Happiness.
The program is a humanitarian journey following more than 10 years of volunteering by Hoa Sen Group. After more than three years of broadcasting in the form of reality television, the program has gone through 28/34 provinces and cities, supporting nearly 500 families to promptly overcome adversity, giving away nearly 71 billion VND and following in the footsteps of more than 1,000 children to continue going to school.
Mr. Nguyen Le Manh Tung - Acting Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group received the award from the Organizing Committee of Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025.
Entering the digital era, over the past three years, Hoa Sen Group has demonstrated its long-term vision by bringing Vietnamese Family Shelter to the digital environment, expanding the audience and increasing the depth of social impact.
With the main idea "Bringing happiness to share with the community", the Vietnamese Family Shelter program is implemented in a combination of corporate social responsibility (CSR) activities and integrated communication strategy. The focus is on a reality TV program periodically broadcast on HTV7, with the participation of famous artists to increase its influence, while focusing on deeply exploiting emotional elements and human stories. Besides traditional television channels, Vietnamese Family Shelter also promotes multi-platform communication on YouTube, Facebook and especially TikTok - where the program creates a strong viral effect with billions of views thanks to short, touching content and the participation of highly influential artists.
At the same time, the highlight of Viet Family Shelter lies in its ability to create an in-depth community communication model. Each story aired is built from real situations, with a true, sophisticated perspective and respect for the characters. The program's production team continuously improves recording, editing and transmission methods to bring inspiring stories to audiences. Thanks to that, the program reaches millions of viewers, creates a strong emotional connection and contributes to nurturing the spirit of sharing in society.
Mr. Nguyen Le Manh Tung - Acting Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group and representatives of honored categories at Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025.
The title at the Vietnam Marketing Awards 2025 is not only an encouragement but also a motivation for Hoa Sen Group and the Vietnamese Family Shelter team to continue to commit to developing programs with social value, contributing to creating positive and long-term impacts for the Vietnamese community.
In particular, in recent years, Hoa Sen Group has also expanded its social impact through the Hoa Sen Home Furniture & Construction Materials supermarket system. With a modern retail model and consumer benefits, Hoa Sen Home not only provides comprehensive solutions for customers' homes but also acts as an extended arm, continuing to carry out the humane mission that Hoa Sen Group has persistently pursued for the past 24 years. At the same time, Hoa Sen Home becomes a bridge for humanistic communication, helping to spread more widely volunteer programs such as "Vietnamese Family Warm Home". This approach demonstrates a marketing strategy that incorporates social responsibility, creates a positive impact and strengthens sustainable brand reputation.

Title: Vietnamese family home is one of 6 typical examples of the emulation movement "For the poor - Leaving no one behind" in the Southern region in 2025
Content:
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program was selected as one of six typical examples in the Emulation Movement "For the poor - Leaving no one behind" in the Southern region in 2025.
On the morning of May 30, at the City Press Center. In Ho Chi Minh City, the Central Emulation and Commendation Committee organized an exchange program of advanced examples in the Emulation Movement "For the poor - Leaving no one behind" in the Southern region in 2025.
This meaningful event aims to honor outstanding individuals and groups, spread creative and effective models in sustainable poverty reduction, contribute to improving people's lives and promoting socio-economic development, helping the poor escape poverty sustainably and stabilize their lives.
Participating in the program were 6 most outstanding groups and individuals interacting directly, sharing experiences, initiatives and inspirational stories in the journey of overcoming difficulties, escaping poverty, getting rich legitimately and supporting the community, contributing practically to the effective implementation of social security policies.
Mr. Nguyen Anh Tuan, Deputy Head of the Central Emulation and Commendation Committee, spoke at the advanced typical exchange program in the emulation movement "For the poor - Leaving no one behind" in the Southern region in 2025.
With a meaningful message about community and a strong spread of love for orphans - "kindergartens" in need of support, support and protection from society, the Vietnamese Family Shelter program once again affirmed its humanistic values and was selected by the Central Emulation and Commendation Committee as one of six typical examples in the Emulation Movement "For the Poor - Leaving no one behind" in 2025 in the Southern region, participating in exchanges. live at the program.
Hoa Sen Group not only considers business activities as its sole goal, but also clearly identifies social responsibility as an inseparable part of the sustainable development process.
The Vietnamese Family Shelter program airs every Friday at 8:20 p.m. on HTV7 channel. The program is carried out by Bee Communications Company in collaboration with Ho Chi Minh City Television Station, with the companionship of Hoa Sen Home Furniture & Construction Materials Supermarket System (Hoa Sen Group).
At the exchange, Mr. Tran Dinh Tai - Acting Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group said: "
The core business philosophy of Hoa Sen Group is: 'Honesty - Community - Development'. During nearly 24 years of establishment and development, we have always considered community as the most important factor. Therefore, in the coming time, Hoa Sen Group will continue to be steadfast on that path, aiming for profound human values through charity activities, social security programs and sharing with difficult circumstances. We believe that a sustainable brand is not only built by product quality or business efficiency, but also by love and responsibility to the community. Hoa Sen Group will continue to spread positive values, accompany society, contributing to bringing happiness, trust and cohesion in every rhythm of Vietnamese life.
”
Mr. Tran Dinh Tai, Acting Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group shared about the story of accompanying orphans and children in difficult circumstances through the "Vietnamese Family Warm Home" program.
For nearly 3 years, Viet Family Shelter has carried out the mission of "bringing happiness to share with the community", always accompanying orphans and children in difficult circumstances across the country. From October 2022 until now, Hoa Sen Group's Vietnamese Family Home has gone through 46 provinces and cities, spreading love to 420 orphan students in difficult circumstances across the country, through scholarship, material, and spiritual support activities... The total value of support from the Group is more than 13 billion VND, and also received the companionship of 269 artists and numerous sponsors, with a total amount of contributions. over 10 billion VND. This is one of the social activities with profound humanity, demonstrating the community responsibility of businesses.
Vietnamese Family Shelter is one of the few reality TV shows about volunteering that receives the attention of leaders and agencies from central to local levels. Having received the Vietnam Creative Advertising Award from the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism for 2 consecutive years, the program continues to be selected by the Central Emulation and Commendation Board to participate in the emulation movement "For the poor - Leaving no one behind" in the southern region in 2025, demonstrating the humane values ​​that the program has brought. After nearly 3 years of broadcasting, Vietnamese Family Shelter has affirmed that it is not only pioneering in creating a new standard for the CSR television trend in Vietnam, but also contributing to arousing and nurturing compassion in society.
Comrade Phan Van Hung, Standing Deputy Head of the Central Emulation and Commendation Committee took souvenir photos with representatives of the Organizing Committee of the Vietnamese Family Shelter program

Second prize
Integrated media advertising
at the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards 2023; Top 1
The most prominent community campaign on construction industry social networks
first half of 2024 and many other awards and nominations.
Each destination on the journey of Vietnamese Family Shelter received much precious love from local audiences.
Vietnam iContent Awards 2024 and especially the Community Organization category with Hoa Sen Group is not only a prestigious award, but also a testament to the continuous efforts of the staff and employees in spreading humanistic values ​​to the community. This achievement is the crystallization of a persistent journey of more than two decades, the business has overcome many challenges to carry out its mission of "bringing happiness to share with the community".

Title: Vietnamese Family Home Honored as “Outstanding Influential Community” at Kotler Awards 2024
Content:
At the Kotler Awards Vietnam 2024 Ceremony, Hoa Sen Group's Vietnamese Family Shelter volunteer program was honored in the category "Excellent Impactful Community" - Community with excellent influence.
Hoa Sen Group's Vietnamese Family Shelter volunteer program has overcome many nominations and excellently won the Excellent Impactful Community award at the Kotler Awards Vietnam 2024
This is the second time the Kotler Awards are held in Vietnam. Kotler Awards is a prestigious international award, founded by Professor Philip Kotler to honor businesses and individuals with outstanding contributions and achievements in the fields of marketing, business, and strategic management. Based on the criteria of the 17 Sustainable Development Goals of the United Nations, this category aims to honor individuals and organizations that have created positive changes for the community through innovative, effective and sustainable development initiatives.
Excellent Impactful Community Award at Kotler Awards Vietnam 2024
At the 2024 Kotler Awards, Hoa Sen Group's Vietnamese Family Shelter volunteer program was honored in the category "Excellent Impactful Community" - Community with excellent influence. This is worthy recognition for the persistent efforts of Hoa Sen Group on the journey to carry out the mission of "bringing happiness to share with the community", spreading the value of love to orphans all over the country. In particular, this award is also a great motivation, urging the team implementing the program to continue their efforts and contribute more to create meaningful projects, contributing to spreading positive and sustainable values ​​to the community.
Kotler Awards 2024 honors outstanding business marketers, marketing experts, strategic managers and businesses with outstanding achievements, contributing to sustainable development and elevating the Vietnamese national brand.
With the mission of "bringing happiness to share with the community", the Vietnamese Family Shelter program has made many impressions in the public's hearts, becoming a charity phenomenon that has received a lot of attention from audiences on social networking, press, and media platforms. Everywhere Viet Family Shelter stopped, the program received a lot of love from local audiences.
Over two years of journey, Vietnamese Family Shelter has achieved achievements such as: Second prize in the category "Integrated media advertising" at the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards 2023 - Award category of the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism; won the category "Brand with meaningful social activities" at the Vietnam Creative Advertising Awards 2023 Honoring Ceremony; Twice entered the voting round at the 2022 and 2023 Mai Vang awards in the category "Most popular program on digital and television platforms". Nominated in the Project category of the Human Act Prize 2023.
Hoa Sen Group's Vietnamese Family Shelter volunteer program has overcome many nominations and excellently won the Excellent Impactful Community award at the Kotler Awards Vietnam 2024
In 2024, the program reached the TOP 1 of the most prominent community campaigns on the Social Network of the Construction - Building Materials industry in the first half of the year; Continuously included in the BSI Top 10 list - Social Media rankings for many consecutive months published by Buzzmetrics. Recently, Vietnamese Family Shelter was nominated in the "Inspirational TV Program" category and Sponsor Hoa Sen Group was nominated in the "Community Organization" category at the Vietnam iContent Awards 2024; continued to receive 4 nominations at the 2024 Vietnam Creativity Awards (Van Xuan Awards).
Up to now, the program has had 116 episodes produced, going through more than 40 provinces and cities and supporting 348 families in difficult circumstances, including many families with up to 4-5 orphan children, lacking the love of their parents.
The total value of bonuses awarded from Hoa Sen Group is more than 11 billion VND. With the support of 230 guests who are famous artists, beauty queens, and athletes from many fields. The amount of money that guests, sponsors and local audiences watched, supported and gave directly to orphans was nearly 6 billion VND. This amount of money has effectively supported families in reducing their economic burden and ensuring school attendance for orphan children.
Vietnamese Family Shelter has successfully spread positive values ​​to millions of audiences.
It can be said that the success of the program comes from the resonance of the sponsors' enthusiasm, the efforts of the production crew, the thoughtful support from local leaders, the noble gestures of the guest artists and especially the kindness of people across the country. It is these actions that together write fairy tales in everyday life, contributing to spreading human values ​​in the community.
Program
Vietnamese family warm home
broadcast every Friday at 8:20 p.m. on HTV7 channel. The program is transmitted by the Company

Title: Vietnamese Family Shelter – Episode 184: Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh join Vietnamese Family Shelter, working hard for the children
Content:
Episode 184 of the program "Vietnamese Family Home" has just aired with the host of MC Thai Vu, with the participation of actor couple Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh. The two artists worked hard to overcome challenges and together with a large number of local audiences contributed more than 250 million VND to children in difficult circumstances.
You
Pham Thi Truc Phuong
(born in 2012), currently an 8th grade student at Nguyen Van Cu Secondary School, Bac Ninh Hoa commune, Khanh Hoa province. Phuong's father died in 2020 due to a traffic accident. In June 2025, my brother also passed away from the same cause. The consecutive departure of two breadwinners left great pain and a void that was difficult to fill for the family.
The family of five now has only three children left, and the lonely meal makes everyone feel speechless
Currently, Phuong lives with her mother, Ms. Nguyen Thi Dung (born in 1987), grandmother Tran Thi Noi (born in 1948) and younger brother Pham Gia Huy (born in 2015), studying in grade 5 at Nguyen Luu Primary School.
Ms. Dung has an eye problem, one eye has lost her sight, the other eye is increasingly deteriorating. After the incident, she alone supported the family, earning a living while taking care of her elderly mother and two small children. Every day, she sells grilled spring rolls on the sidewalk from 2pm to 9pm, earning about 100,000–150,000 VND. It depends a lot on the weather, so in the rainy season there is almost no income.
Phuong's two sisters help their mother sell after every class. Phuong's sisters help their mother sell after every class
Grandmother's health is poor, she can only do odd jobs and receive an allowance of 500,000 VND per month. The family also has to pay for electricity, water and other living expenses, often falling short, and currently owes about 5 million VND.
Understanding her mother's hardships, Truc Phuong helps sell goods, do housework, and take care of her. I still haven't gotten over the pain of losing my father and brother. It was those losses that made Phuong love her mother even more, hoping that in the future she would be able to buy her a new set of clothes - something that Dung had not been able to take care of herself for a long time. Phuong is determined to study well so that in the future she can take care of her mother and grandmother and become a support for her family.
MC Thai Vu gave words of encouragement and comfort to the child's family
Following the story, MC Thai Vu could not hide his sadness when in just 5 years, Truc Phuong's family lost two pillars. The image of the mother with her eyes gradually losing sight, still struggling to make a living to take care of her elderly mother and young child made the male MC choked up.
When hearing Gia Huy share his dream of becoming a doctor to treat his mother's eyes, Cris Phan could not contain his emotions. He sent words of encouragement to his family and encouraged Gia Huy to continue his efforts in studying, because he is the only man and the future fulcrum of the family.
Mai Quynh Anh also teared up when Truc Phuong talked about her father. Saying:
"When I see my friends with my father by their side, I miss my father and all I can do is go home and burn incense for him."
made the actress choked up. She expressed her admiration for the children's understanding by studying diligently, helping the family, always thinking about their mothers and grandmothers, and raising the hope of growing up soon to take care of the family.
Not only stopping at emotional shares, Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh also directly "rolled over" in the challenges. In the consecutive competition, the two overcame many obstacles to bring the ball to the finish line, then quickly moved to the "mobile supermarket" of Hoa Sen Home to find and buy all 20 products as required. The challenge requires agility, sharp eyes and coordination, and gives students an advantage in the main round.
In another part of the competition, two artists and characters must find balls hidden in a tub of powder, move them and hit the target. Even though they were tired and had their clothes and faces covered in white powder, the two still persevered in completing the task with the hope of bringing back valuable rewards for the children.
Nguyen Cam Uyen's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 62 million VND
Through the competitions, Pham Thi Truc Phuong's family finished third, receiving 19 million VND; Tran Thi Hong Tham's family finished second with 23 million VND; And Nguyen Cam Uyen's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 62 million VND.
Thus, Hoa Sen Group awarded a total of 104 million VND to three families this week
.
In addition, the artists also used their own money, along with sponsors and a large number of local audiences to support the families. It is estimated that the total amount of support from the program along with contributions from artists and sponsors for children this week is up to more than 250 million VND.
Tuy Hoa's audience stayed until the end of the recording session, giving more motivation to the three artists and the children

Vietnamese family warm home
