Title: Vietnamese Family Home – Episode 184: Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh are heartbroken at the sight of two orphan girls relying on each other in the old house left by their parents.
Content:
Cris Phan, Mai Quynh Anh and MC Thai Vu could not hide their sadness when witnessing the story of two orphaned sisters relying on each other in an old house and a 12th grade female student supporting her family in poverty but still striving to study.
Thai Vu, Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh are eager to participate in the program, ready to accompany and spread love to children in difficult circumstances.
Episode 184 of the program “
Vietnamese family warm home
” is about to air with the hosting of MC Thai Vu, along with the companionship of two guests, actor couple Cris Phan - Mai Quynh Anh. At the beginning of the program, Mai Quynh Anh shared her happiness when accompanying the program right in her hometown (former Phu Yen) - where she was born and raised. Meanwhile, Cris Phan said he had watched the show before and couldn't hide his emotions when listening to the stories about the characters' situations.
The actor was especially impressed by the image of a large audience present at the recording session, not only to meet the artist but also to directly donate cash and in-kind support to the families. He said he will do his best in the challenges, not only hoping to help the children complete the exam but also contributing to motivating them to overcome difficulties in life.
Four orphaned sisters relied on each other after the incident, the eldest sister worked far away to take care of the younger siblings
You
Nguyen Cam Uyen
(born in 2008), currently an 11th grade student at Nguyen Du High School No. 2. Uyen lost her father when she was just 1 year old. After that, her mother took another step and gave birth to Tran Bien Hieu Thao (born in 2014). In 2020, my stepfather left and passed away a few years later. Tragedy continued when her mother suffered from leukemia for a long time and passed away in November 2023. Uyen currently lives with her younger brother Thao in Uar commune, Gia Lai province.
Orphaned by both parents, Cam Uyen had to study and work to make ends meet and take care of her younger sister.
Uyen also has two older sisters, Nguyen Huu Thanh Tam (born in 2003) and Nguyen Huu Thanh Mai (born in 2005). After their mother passed away, the four sisters relied on each other in the old house their parents left behind, trying to continue their studies. At the time of her mother's death, Thanh Tam was preparing to go to college but put her dream aside and went to Ho Chi Minh City to work as a worker to take care of her younger siblings. Every month, she saves about 2 million VND to make ends meet.
The second sister, Thanh Mai, is married and has a 6-month-old child. Due to her own difficult circumstances, her husband works as a worker with little income, so when she saves, she can support her two younger siblings.
Despite their poor circumstances, Uyen and Thao are both obedient and studious. To support her family, Uyen took advantage of many jobs outside of school hours such as working at a coffee shop, typing documents, and working in the fields when someone hired her. Belonging to a poor household, without their parents, the two sisters still persistently pursued their studies. Uyen has been an excellent student for many years, cherishing the dream of becoming a teacher. Losing her parents at an early age made her even more determined to study well to take care of her younger sister and reduce the burden on her eldest sister.
The two sisters always miss their mother but rarely mention it because they are afraid that Tam will feel more heartbroken. Uyen told herself that she would try her best to study so that in the future she could take care of her younger sister, so that her older sister could live for her own happiness.
Cris Phan and Mai Quynh Anh were heartbroken when they learned that the girl always blamed herself for not growing up enough to earn money to treat her mother's illness.
Following the character's situation, MC Thai Vu couldn't help but feel sad when he saw that the small house had lost both parents, and the four sisters could only rely on each other. The image of Uyen and Thao living in the old house when the eldest sister worked far away made the male MC choked up.
When hearing Uyen blame herself for not having grown up enough to earn money to treat her mother's illness, Thai Vu emotionally comforted her:
"Don't ever think that it was because of you that my mother died. I always thought of good things for my children, so I hid my illness. I love my children very much and I don't want to see them blame themselves like that."
The male MC also expressed his admiration for the sacrifice of Thanh Tam - the eldest sister who put aside her dreams to support the family.
Actress Mai Quynh Anh was moved by her eldest sister's commitment and sacrifice for her children. The image of sisters Uyen and Thao holding hands while participating in the program made her choke up. The actress expressed her admiration for the fact that the children had experienced many losses when they were young, but still did not give up studying and always tried to improve.
Actor Cris Phan also expressed his respect for the strength of the two sisters. He believes that, even though they face many difficulties and deprivation, they still maintain a positive spirit. The eldest sister's sacrifice and the children's will to study made the actor both admire and feel sad. I send words of encouragement, hoping you will continue to try and not blame yourself.
Witnessing the situation of two sisters living in poor conditions, not even having a toilet so they had to wait until late at night to dare to do personal activities, Mr. Tran Dinh Tai, Deputy General Director of Hoa Sen Group, on behalf of the sponsor, Hoa Sen Home Furniture & Construction Materials Supermarket System, decided to build a new toilet. In addition, he also donated personal money to give Uyen a bicycle, hoping to motivate her to continue.

Title: Vietnamese Family Shelter – Episode 183: Nearly 340 million VND was given to difficult situations in episode 183 of the Vietnamese Family Shelter program
Content:
Episode 183 of the program "Vietnamese Family Home" just aired, MC Thanh Thao, athlete Truong Dinh Hoang and singer Mai Phuong could not hide their emotions at the difficult situation of the three sisters Ngoc Diem, living in poverty with the fear of losing their father to serious illness, and their mother working hard day and night to take care of the family.
You
Le Anh Ngoc Diem
(born in 2009), currently an 11th grade student at Phan Chau Trinh High School No. 1, Ea Drang commune, Dak Lak province. I live with my father, Le Xuan Dinh (born in 1976), mother, Le Thi Thuy (born in 1979), and two younger sisters: Le Thi Thao Nguyen (born in 2014, in 6th grade) and Le Bao Thi (born in 2016, in 4th grade).
After school, Diem helps her mother with housework and takes care of her father who has Parkinson's disease
Ngoc Diem's family is poor and has no land for production. My father has severe Parkinson's disease with movement disorders, making walking and daily activities extremely difficult. In 2018, when her husband got sick, Ms. Thuy sold 7 acres of land - the family's largest asset - to treat her husband's illness, but it was ineffective and his illness got worse. Each month, the cost of medicine ranges from 1.5 to 2 million VND, sometimes not enough for the entire month.
Her husband is seriously ill, so Ms. Thuy has to work hard from morning to night to support the whole family. During the day, she weeds, picks coffee, and picks pepper with an income of 200 - 250 thousand VND/day; In the evening, he takes the opportunity to wash dishes by the hour, earning an unstable income. Even though she was often in pain and tired, she did not dare go to the doctor because she was worried that she would not be able to take care of her family. Faced with that situation, the Women's Union of Ea Drang commune supported the family with two breeding pigs and a small barn, creating a long-term livelihood.
Previously, the family lived in a dilapidated and ramshackle house. Recently, thanks to a support of 9 million VND and an additional loan of 25 million VND, the family was able to build the front part of the house. The back part is still not solid and does not have a toilet, so they had to go to a neighbor.
In a deprived situation, Ngoc Diem is still obedient, understanding and tries her best to study with the dream of becoming a fashion designer. Outside of school, I work with my mother, help with housework, take care of pigs, and tutor younger siblings to share the burden. Seeing her father sick, Diem felt very sorry for him but only tried to study well and help with housework. For me, as long as my father is happier and my mother has less hardship, all the efforts are worth it.
At the studio, the three sisters burst into tears when talking about their family situation
MC Thanh Thao could not hide her sadness when she witnessed the three Ngoc Diem sisters burst into tears, sharing their worries that they might lose their father in the future. She also expressed her admiration for Ms. Thuy's diligence and determination in still trying to earn money to take care of her husband and children even though her husband had lost his ability to work. The mother's silent sacrifice touched the female MC.
Listening to Ngoc Diem talk about the debt of more than 10 million VND that could not be paid off for many years, and the fact that her mother had to sell the only piece of land to treat her father's illness, Thanh Thao became even more heartbroken. She shared that before each program recording, she often proactively meets friends and calls for support in difficult situations. Right at the program, the female MC deducted 10 million VND to help Ngoc Diem's ​​family pay off their debt, making the girl emotionally cry.
Athlete Truong Dinh Hoang could not hold back his tears as he watched the story of the three sisters. He expressed his sadness at the disadvantages the children had to endure, and at the same time admired the children's will to rise up and their understanding. The male athlete decided to support the family with 10 million VND and promised to visit and give more practical gifts to help the children stabilize their lives.
Meanwhile, singer Mai Phuong also choked up at the situation of Ngoc Diem and her children. In particular, when she heard Diem's ​​younger sister share that her dream was not for herself but only for her parents to be healthy and live a long life with her child, she felt even more sad. The female singer was touched by the sacrifice of Ms. Thuy, a mother who diligently took care of her family, working so hard that when she was sick, she did not dare to buy medicine, did not dare to eat enough, and saved every little bit to take care of her children.
Witnessing Ngoc Diem's ​​family's situation, which is still lacking, the father is seriously ill, the children are young but the house does not have a personal toilet, the representative of Hoa Sen Group said that they will support the construction of a new toilet to make it convenient for the family in daily activities.
Singer Mai Phuong "forgot her image" and crawled on soapy water
Not only did the artists bring words of encouragement and encouragement, they also devoted all their efforts to conquering the challenges of the program. Athlete Truong Dinh Hoang, despite being injured, still strives to complete his assigned tasks. Meanwhile, singer Mai Phuong is not afraid to roll: in a challenge of crawling on soapy water, she was ready to throw her whole body, regardless of dirty clothes, to overcome the obstacle.
Notably, not only taking on the leading role, MC Thanh Thao also "broke the rules" and directly participated in the challenge with guest artists. The female MC is not afraid to get wet and dirty when crawling on soapy water with Mai Phuong and Truong Dinh Hoang. Everyone tried their best to complete the challenge in the shortest time, with the hope of bringing back meaningful gifts for the children.
Gi

Title: Vietnamese Family Home – Episode 183: Truong Dinh Hoang was touched by the filial boy who studied while working to take care of his sick mother for more than 10 years
Content:
Two particularly difficult situations: a 9th grade boy studying while mowing lawns to support his sick mother, and a 12th grade girl who was an orphan early on to support her family, made MC Thanh Thao and the artists unable to contain their emotions, when their energy and desire to rise far outweighed the deprivations and losses in life.
Thanh Thao, Mai Phuong, and Truong Dinh Hoang were heartbroken by the strength of the less fortunate children
At the beginning of the program, MC Thanh Thao expressed her appreciation for the companionship of the local audience. She shared:
"Even though the program was recorded at 1pm and it was very sunny, the people here were very enthusiastic when they came to cheer for the children today. The people who came here not only cheered but also brought a spirit of sharing, giving gifts to accompany the children, giving them more motivation to overcome the challenges of the program as well as the difficulties in life."
Singer Mai Phuong said she was always excited and wanted to join the program early to have the opportunity to help children and families in difficult circumstances, and continue the philanthropic journey that previous guest artists had made. The female singer expressed her impression with the image of artists who are not afraid to get dirty, ready to do their best to complete challenges, providing practical support for the characters. That made her even more determined, telling herself she would try her best when participating in the program.
Second time coming
Vietnamese family warm home
, athlete Truong Dinh Hoang said he always wanted to come back to accompany and share with children in difficult circumstances. At the recording session, the male athlete could not hide his emotions when witnessing unlucky lives. Even though he had just experienced a traffic accident that resulted in three broken ribs, he still decided to participate after a period of consideration, with the desire to contribute to sharing with children who are in need both materially and mentally.
The 9th grade boy soon took care of his family, studying and cutting grass to help his grandmother take care of his sick mother
Luong Tan Dat (born in 2011), currently a 9th grade student at Nguyen Anh Hao Secondary School, lives with his grandmother, mother and brother in Phu Yen ward, Dak Lak province. Growing up without his father's love and his mother suffering from severe depression, Dat relied on his grandmother's care from a young age.
Luong Tan Dat - a 9th grade boy who studies and works at the same time, dreams of becoming a teacher
Dat's mother has been depressed for more than 10 years, is mentally unstable, and when she gets sick, she often screams and commits violent and dangerous behavior. The family had to let her stay in a small house next to her grandmother's house to take care of her. Since childhood, Dat did not receive complete care from his mother, growing up in the arms of his grandmother, Mrs. Le Thi Suong (born in 1952). Due to her old age and weak health, she suffers from many diseases and cannot do heavy labor. The whole family lives on an allowance from mother and child of about 1,200,000 VND per month, along with support from benefactors of 500,000 VND every 3 months.
Despite her illness, Mrs. Suong still tries her best to take care of her children and grandchildren, but is always worried that if she passes away, there will be no one left to take care of Dat. Therefore, she often encourages her to try to study and live well. To earn extra income, she grows vegetables to sell, earning about 30,000 VND every few days. The family still has 1 acres of land, thanks to their son-in-law for farming, the food is only enough to last the day.
Difficult circumstances forced Dat's older half-brother, Luong Huynh Tan Thanh (born in 2009), to leave school early and go to Da Nang to find a job. The job is unstable, the income is only enough to take care of herself, and only occasionally sends her a few hundred thousand dong. Soon aware of the situation, Dat took advantage of the weekend to cut grass for rent, earning 100,000 VND each day and gave it all to her to cover living expenses.
The house the family is living in was built by their grandmother in 1983 and is now seriously degraded. During the big flood in November 2025, water flooded up to the roof, the whole family had to break the roof and climb up for temporary shelter. Even though he lacks motherly love, Dat still loves, sympathizes and hopes to grow up soon so he can take care of and compensate for his grandmother and mother.
MC Thanh Thao choked up and hugged Dat tightly when she knew that her only dream was just a hug from her mother
MC Thanh Thao gave many compliments to Dat's understanding when he learned to help his family early, and when he was young, he worked part-time to help his grandmother. Despite difficult circumstances, she still did not give up studying and always strived to improve, making the female MC extremely impressed. Thanh Thao could not hide her sadness when witnessing Dat's poor life, especially when she had to face the times when her mother got sick. The emotions heightened when Dat shared his simple wish for his mother to be healthy and alert so she could hug her just once. Before those confessions, the female MC burst into tears, hugged and encouraged the boy.
Singer Mai Phuong choked up when she saw that Dat was so young but had to shoulder many responsibilities. She said that at her age, many people only focus on studying, while Dat studies, works, and takes care of his grandmother and mother. When she heard Dat share her wish to have a family meal full of members, the female singer became even more emotional.
Athlete Truong Dinh Hoang also expressed his admiration for Dat's affection and filial piety. He said what made him emotional

Title: Viet Family's Home - Episode 182: The woman took care of the boy for more than 10 years, making Dai Nghia burst into tears on television
Content:
Episode 182 of the program "Vietnamese Family Home" just aired, MC Dai Nghia, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu and singer Phuong Anh Bolero could not hide their emotions at the gesture of "grandmother Hien" - who for more than 10 years took care of her neighbor's son when his mother was away from home for treatment.
Program
Vietnamese family warm home
Episode 182 was hosted by MC Dai Nghia, with the participation of two guests, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu and singer Phuong Anh Bolero. The artists overcome challenges to support three difficult situations: Ta Quang Phu (born in 2013), Nguyen Thao Van (born in 2012) and Tran Khai Minh (born in 2012).
In particular, the situation of Tran Khai Minh brought many tears from the audience. Khai Minh is currently an 8th grade student, Tran Cao Van Secondary School, living in Binh Kien ward, Dak Lak province. I don't have a father and have had to live with my neighbors for many years.
Every time she mentioned her mother, Minh's eyes filled with tears because of her deep nostalgia
Minh's mother, Ms. Tran Thi Phuong (born in 1990), has lupus erythematosus and currently rents a room in Ho Chi Minh City for convenient treatment at Cho Ray Hospital. Living with Ms. Phuong are Minh's grandmother and uncle, who has both legs. Even though she suffered from goiter, her grandmother still went with her son to sell lottery tickets every day to make ends meet and saved up a small sum to send home to help with Minh's living expenses.
During nearly 10 years of treatment away from home, Minh's mother never came back to visit her child because of her poor health. The last time mother and daughter met was about two years ago when Minh was brought to Ho Chi Minh City by her grandmother. Since then, I have only contacted my mother by phone and am always afraid that I will never have the chance to see her again.
Minh's grandmother and uncle traveled around selling lottery tickets, illuminating their grandchildren in their hometown
In his hometown, Minh still has his grandfather, but he is old, weak, and works as a seafarer, so he only comes home every few months. Minh's two other uncles currently have tuberculosis and are being treated at Pham Ngoc Thach Hospital. All medical expenses for the children are shouldered by the grandfather.
Feeling sorry for her situation, Ms. Nguyen Thi Hien - Head of the Women's Union of Binh Kien ward accepted to take care of Khai Minh like a descendant of the family. I call her "Grandma Hien", always love and respect her like a close relative. Not only does she take care of daily activities, she also teaches me how to live kindly and orients me to become a useful person.
Khai Minh is an obedient, understanding boy who often helps with housework while living with "grandmother Hien". At one point, seeing how hard it was for my family, I thought about quitting school to go to work, but my grandmother encouraged me to continue going to school. Currently, each month, the family receives an allowance of 700,000 VND from his mother's illness policy, sent by his grandfather to support Minh's living expenses.
Despite many hardships, Minh still dreams of becoming a lawyer, hoping to earn money to treat his mother's illness, take care of "grandmother Hien" and help people in difficult circumstances.
Witnessing the story, MC Dai Nghia could not hold back his tears. He expressed his respect for the heart of "grandmother Hien":
"There are stories that make us cry because of heartache, but there are also stories that make us cry because of warmth. The story of 'grandmother Hien' is such a story."
The male MC said he was especially impressed that Mrs. Hien had taken care of Minh for more than 10 years - a journey that was not easy. He also praised Khai Minh's understanding, and expressed concern for the health of his mother and grandmother who are struggling to make a living in Ho Chi Minh City.
Singer Nguyen Dinh Vu also choked up when listening to the story. He believes that taking care of a child is not easy, but raising a child who is not related by blood for many years is even more admirable. The male singer expressed his admiration for Ms. Hien's heart and was touched by Khai Minh's eagerness to learn and his will to improve.
The mother's handwritten letter to her son made the whole studio burst into tears
Before the recording session, Minh's mother sent a letter asking the crew to deliver it to her child. The messages read right at the program touched many people. Singer Phuong Anh Bolero could not hide his tears and hugged Khai Minh to encourage him. She sent her deep thanks to "grandmother Hien" and encouraged Minh to continue his efforts to study and pursue his dream.
Besides words of encouragement, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu and singer Phuong Anh Bolero also tried their best in movement challenges, contributing to bringing valuable rewards to children in difficult circumstances. Through the competitions, Ta Quang Phu's family finished third, receiving 17 million VND; Nguyen Thao Van's family finished second with 22 million VND; Tran Khai Minh's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 60 million VND.
Thus, Hoa Sen Group awarded a total of 99 million VND to three families this week
.
Tran Khai Minh's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 60 million VND from Hoa Sen Group.
In addition, singer Nguyen Dinh Vu donated 10 million VND from his pocket to each family. Similarly, singer Phuong Anh Bolero donated 10 million VND to each family. Many sponsors and local people also contributed

Title: Vietnamese Family Home – Episode 182: MC Dai Nghia burst into tears at the 13-year-old boy who blamed himself for being a burden that made his parents leave.
Content:
From the question "Is it true that the child is not good so the parents leave?" During those nights alone in a poor motel room, the story of two children made MC Dai Nghia, Nguyen Dinh Vu, Phuong Anh Bolero unable to hold back their tears.
A 13-year-old boy lives with his elderly grandparents, always tormented because he thinks he is a burden
Ta Quang Phu (born in 2013), is currently a 7th grade student at Nguyen Trung Truc Secondary School. My parents divorced when I was young. Father got married and mother went to work far away. Phu's grandparents lived elsewhere, their economic conditions were unstable, so they were unable to support them.
Phu currently lives with his maternal great-grandfather, Mr. Huynh Van Tam (born in 1949) and maternal great-grandmother, Huynh Thi Thuy Lieu (born in 1953) in Tu Bong commune, Khanh Hoa province. Due to their advanced age, weak health, and no longer being able to work, the couple only relies on a monthly allowance of about 1.5 million VND (including support for the disabled and the elderly) to cover living expenses for the whole family.
In the small house, there was only Phu and the two grandparents quietly relying on each other
My great-grandfather had blurred vision, weak legs, had difficulty walking, and was almost unable to function normally on his own; My great-grandmother was also often sick. All daily activities are mainly thanks to the aunt who occasionally comes to support, especially the heavy work that the grandparents cannot do themselves.
The family lives in a small house near the highway and raises a few chickens to improve their meals. The house was built more than 20 years ago. Even though the foundation was repaired in May 2025, the roof has deteriorated and often leaks when it rains.
Without her mother's love, Phu does not blame her but loves her even more, always wishing her to have happiness and a perfect family. For me, being cared for by my great-grandparents is a great happiness.
Since childhood, Phu has never had a meal full of family members, but she still feels lucky because she always has her grandparents by her side, loving and taking care of her. My grandparents not only nurture me but are also my biggest spiritual support.
What worries Phu the most is that his grandparents' health is deteriorating. I'm afraid that one day I won't be able to stay with my grandparents until I grow up. Seeing his elderly grandparents still struggling to support him, Phu felt sad but didn't know what to do to help. Sometimes he had low self-esteem, thought he was a burden and thought that because he wasn't good enough, his parents weren't around.
Even so, I still have the dream of getting a good education and having a stable job to take care of my grandparents. What I hope most is for my grandparents to always be healthy and stay with me for a long time so I can have the opportunity to show my filial piety.
All three artists felt sad when they learned that Phu always blamed himself and thought that he was the burden that caused his parents to leave
MC Dai Nghia could not hide his emotions when listening to Phu's story. He choked up and shared:
"There are children who understand so much that it hurts. At the age of 13, Phu has never fully felt the love of his parents. What torments me the most is when he wonders, 'Is it because I'm not good that my parents left me?'. But there is no child who grows up without crying."
The male MC gave many words of encouragement, hoping Phu would not blame himself. He expressed his emotion when he saw that the boy always sympathized with his parents, and at the same time hoped that Phu's parents, no matter how busy they are, will take the time to care for and be with their child. According to him, at this age, Phu still needs love and protection from his family.
Singer Nguyen Dinh Vu expressed his sadness when witnessing Phu's situation. Not only do I lack affection, I also have to rely on my great-grandparents who are elderly and no longer able to work. Her words:
"Whenever I don't have money, I don't eat or take medicine."
made the male singer choked up.
Singer Phuong Anh Bolero hugged Phu tightly, continuously comforting him when he burst into tears. The singer admired the filial piety of the boy who knew how to do housework and take care of his grandparents when he was young. She also couldn't hide her heartbreak when she witnessed the situation in her family where no one else had the strength to work but still relied on and protected each other every day.
Without her parents by her side, the 15-year-old girl agreed to live alone to continue going to school
Nguyen Thao Van (born in 2012), is currently an 8th grade student at Suoi Bac Secondary School, Son Hoa commune, Dak Lak province. I don't have a father, my mother left when Van was only 15 months old, and only occasionally calls home. Since childhood, I grew up under the care of my grandmother, Mrs. Nguyen Thi Hong (born 1958) in Van Hoa commune.
Even though she lacked her mother's love, Van did not blame her but always understood. She said her mother has mental problems, sometimes she is not alert so she cannot control her behavior. Therefore, I just silently love and hope to see my mother again one day when she is healthier.
To raise her grandchildren, Mrs. Hong works as an agricultural laborer. Everyone does what they do, such as pulling cassava, cutting grass, and harvesting crops. However, her health increasingly weakened after eye and knee surgeries. The family is near poor and has no regular source of support other than health insurance, making life always in a state of shortage.
A rented room far from home and her dream of filial piety for her grandmother
The house is more than 10km from school and there is no means of transportation. In order for Van to continue studying, her grandmother had to send her to stay at an acquaintance's house near the school. Since then, Van has lived far away from her

Title: Vietnamese Family Shelter – Episode 181: MC Duong Hong Phuc was surprised by actress Thanh Huong's "law-breaking" performance in Vietnamese Family Shelter
Content:
Episode 181 of the program "Vietnamese Family Home" has just aired with the host of MC Duong Hong Phuc, with the participation of Meritorious Artist Duc Khue and actress Thanh Huong. The two artists worked hard to overcome challenges and together with a large local audience contributed more than 330 million VND to children in difficult circumstances.
Tran Thanh Thuy (born in 2014), currently a 6th grade student at Phong Du Primary and Secondary School, Tien Yen commune, Quang Ninh province, lives with her father, Tran Van Hung (born in 1982), half-brother Tran Duc Khiem (born in 2009) and twin sister Tran Phuong Thuy.
Thuy's mother passed away in 2019 due to lung disease. At that time, the twin sisters were too young to fully understand the loss. Since my mother passed away, the already difficult home has become even more empty. For more than 6 years, Mr. Hung alone shouldered the role of both father and mother, quietly raising three children in poor conditions.
The hard-working father Tran Van Hung quietly raised his three children in poor circumstances for many years.
To take care of his children's education, Mr. Hung did many jobs locally such as farming, growing acacia, peeling acacia for hire, loading and unloading... Whenever there was a job, he accepted it, not afraid of hard work. However, the precarious income from unskilled labor jobs is still not enough to ensure a living for a family of four. Mr. Hung's health is not good, he has stomach disease and often has bone and joint pain when doing heavy work. Loving his young children, he did not work far away but only took a job near home to conveniently take care of them.
The family has a small field, produces two rice crops a year, and earns only a few million dong after deducting expenses. Most of the rice is kept as food. Tran Duc Khiem is currently in 11th grade, is his wife's stepchild, but is always loved by Mr. Hung like his own child. Khiem once expressed his dream of going to university but was worried that his family would not be able to afford it, fearing that his father would face more hardship. Listening to his son, Hung just remained silent, hoping he could take care of his son and give him a better future.
Understanding their father's hardships, all three brothers were obedient and knew how to help with housework after school. Khiem doesn't have his own car, so he has to help a friend get to school, and Thuy and her sisters ride each other every day on an old bicycle.
Even though life is still lacking, the small house is still warm and full of love. The three children grew up in difficulty but were compensated by the sacrifice and love of their father, who always devoted his whole life to them.
MC Duong Hong Phuc was touched by the children's understanding and filial piety, and admired Mr. Hung's efforts in doing everything to take care of his children, especially his love for Duc Khiem even though he is not his biological child. Mr. Hung's tears when talking about his children, despite his declining health, made the male MC choke up.
Two artists Thanh Huong and Duc Khue tried their best in the challenge of bringing the ball to the finish line, determined to bring rewards to the children.
Actress Thanh Huong was moved by the situation of "a rooster raising children", expressing her admiration for Hung's strength and the children's spirit of overcoming difficulties. The actress hugged Thanh Thuy and her sisters in her arms, hoping to share some of her love with them who soon lacked motherly love. She decided to give all her salary to her family and give each child a new pair of earrings after learning that the two children had lost their mother's souvenir earrings.
Meritorious Artist Duc Khue also expressed his admiration for Mr. Hung's resilience, and sent words of encouragement for him to maintain his health to continue being a support for his children. The male artist reminded the children to be good, study hard, love and care for each other towards a better future.
Not only stopping at encouragement, the artists also tried their best in challenges to bring prize money to the children. In the secondary challenge, Thanh Huong and Meritorious Artist Duc Khue had to use a hand-held fan to send a chicken-shaped balloon over an obstacle to the finish line. Both ran and fanned the ball with great difficulty to complete.
Tran Thanh Thuy's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 58 million VND.
However, MC Duong Hong Phuc unexpectedly did not recognize the results, saying that Thanh Huong "cheated". The actress immediately reacted, saying that if she made a mistake, she needed to be reminded to do it right. When asked to return the ball to the starting line, she also asked the MC to turn away to reduce pressure. Meanwhile, Thanh Huong took advantage of holding many balls at the same time instead of moving each ball one by one, even "inviting" Meritorious Artist Duc Khue to do it together to quickly complete the task, gaining an advantage for the children. Even though he is under 60 years old, Meritorious Artist Duc Khue is still dedicated to the challenge of movement, trying to overcome fatigue to bring meaningful rewards to the children.
Through the competitions, Bui Hai Dang's family finished third, receiving 18 million VND; Ngo Minh Duong's family finished second with 22 million VND; Tran Thanh Thuy's family won first prize, overcoming special challenges and bringing home a total of 58 million VND.
Thus, Hoa Sen Group awarded a total of 98 million VND to three families this week
.
In addition, the artists also used their own money, along with sponsors and a large number of local audiences to support the families. Estimated total amount of support from the same program

Title: Vietnamese Family Home – Episode 181: Meritorious Artist Duc Khue and actress Thanh Huong were moved by the image of mothers struggling to raise their children after their husbands passed away.
Content:
Episode 181 of the Vietnamese Family Warm program has just aired with the host of MC Duong Hong Phuc, with the participation of two guests, Meritorious Artist Duc Khue and actress Thanh Huong. The artists could not hide their sadness at the image of mothers struggling to make a living, raising their children alone after their husbands passed away.
As soon as he appeared in the city. Hai Phong, Meritorious Artist Duc Khue excited the audience when singing the song
City of red phoenix flowers
with familiar lyrics: "The sky is filled with red poinciana flowers in May. Oh Hai Phong, my hometown...". Meanwhile, actress Thanh Huong also performed a song
Sing about Hai Duong
, highlighting a part of the city. New Hai Phong.
Thanh Huong shared her happiness when returning to her hometown of Hai Phong to film
Vietnamese family warm home
. The actress said that this was the second time she participated in the program, after the first time in Nghe An, and expressed her desire to continue accompanying in filming sessions in the South to contribute to spreading humanistic values ​​across the country.
Meritorious Artist Duc Khue and actress Thanh Huong accompany Vietnamese Family Shelter, spreading the message of love to families in difficult circumstances.
Meritorious Artist Duc Khue could not hide his emotions when returning to the program for the second time. The male artist shared his philanthropic journey
Vietnamese family warm home
has supported many children in difficult circumstances. He expressed his pride in participating and was ready to accept participation as soon as invited, even though the program's challenges require high physical strength.
It's sad that a mother and child both have heart disease and rely on each other in a dilapidated house
Bui Hai Dang (born in 2012) is currently an 8th grade student at Duong Lam Secondary School, Son Tay ward, Hanoi city. My father passed away in 2018 due to cirrhosis. Hai Dang currently lives with her mother, Phan Thi Yen (born in 1984). Both mother and daughter have congenital heart disease.
Since her husband passed away, Yen has become the family's breadwinner. Even though she had a heart disease since childhood and was in poor health, she still tried to make a living. When her illness was not serious, she worked as a construction assistant to increase her income. Currently, she works at a banh te shop near her house, earning about 150,000 VND/day but is unstable, only working 2-3 times a week outside of the holiday season.
Hai Dang is skinny and often tired due to illness but still tries to study and take care of his mother.
Uncertain income makes treatment of heart valve regurgitation difficult. Even though the doctor asked her to have regular check-ups every 2-3 months, she only went to the doctor when she had enough money, and otherwise maintained taking medicine every day at a cost of about 1,200,000 VND/month.
The illness causes her to often have back pain, difficulty walking, and the inability to sit for long periods of time. Every time I stand up or sit down, I feel dizzy and dizzy. She always wanted to be able to earn money for medical treatment to prolong the time she takes to care for her children, but that remains unfinished when the family does not have a stable source of income. There were times when there was no money left in the house, so mother and child had to go to their grandmother's house to ask for food to make ends meet. When talking about the current situation, she just remained silent and helpless.
Hai Dang also suffered from heart disease inherited from his mother, was diagnosed with pulmonary valve regurgitation, had frequent chest tightness, difficulty breathing, and had to be exempted from physical education classes. Because of difficult circumstances, she did not receive full treatment and had to share medicine with her mother, sharing each pill.
Poor health makes Hai Dang's body thin and pale. Understanding my situation, teachers support breakfast and school supplies. Life's lack of before and after makes me rarely have new clothes. Hai Dang only wanted to be able to buy his mother a portion of broken rice - her favorite dish - and a new car to go to school, but he always kept it a secret because he was afraid that his mother would not have money.
The house where the mother and child live was built in 1995 and is now seriously degraded. The old tile roof leaks in many places, every time it rains there is almost no dry place left. At times like that, mother and daughter just put foam sheets on the bed and hugged each other to wait for the rain to stop. The house has no toilet or bathroom; The temporary toilet area is located outdoors, and bathing must be done in an uncovered corner of the kitchen. Because his mother cannot carry heavy loads, every day Hai Dang makes and carries warm water for her to bathe.
The artists couldn't help but feel sorry for Hai Dang's mother and daughter, who rely on each other in a rundown house, fighting together with congenital heart disease.
Actress Thanh Huong expressed her sadness at the situation of Hai Dang's family, which was already difficult when they lost their breadwinner, but is now struggling even more due to illness. The actress is especially worried about the health condition of mother and child, as Dang is still too young but is already facing heart disease. She hopes the community will join hands to help her child have conditions for treatment and lead a better life, and at the same time support 5 million VND to share some of the difficulties.
MC Duong Hong Phuc could not hide his emotions at the family's eventful situation: losing his father, having a sick mother, and having just fallen, causing his health to deteriorate further. He expressed his admiration that Ms. Yen, even though life is sometimes too difficult, still tries every day for her children. Hai Dang's understanding and maturity also touched the male MC when he not only studied hard but also helped his mother, carried water, and took care of the housework.
Meritorious Artist Duc Khue also shared his sadness
